info.dc:title: singine-edge
info.dc:creator: Sina K. Heshmati
info.dc:subject: Collibra Edge lifecycle and Kubernetes file staging
info.dc:description: singine edge — manage Collibra Edge and stage local files into the live Kubernetes runtime
info.dc:rights: Copyright 2026 Sina K. Heshmati. All rights reserved.

refnamediv.refpurpose: build, deploy, verify, and operate the Collibra Edge stack

cmdsynopsis.command: singine collibra edge create datasource connection

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: singine edge is the operator command surface for the Collibra Edge stack. In the current Kubernetes deployment, the most useful repeatable workflow is staging local files from the workstation into the running Edge runtime so later ingestion jobs can consume them.
The default staging target is the collibra-edge-objects-server pod in namespace collibra-edge. The default remote path is /tmp/singine.
The corresponding repeatable database workflow is to import the local SQLite file into PostgreSQL and then generate the exact datasource values to paste into the Collibra Edge UI.

listitem.para: Import the SQLite database into PostgreSQL:
listitem.programlisting: python3 -m singine.command pg create database fromSQLite /tmp/singine/sqlite.db --db-name singine_bridge --replace

listitem.para: Print the Collibra Edge datasource values:
listitem.programlisting: python3 -m singine.command collibra edge create datasource connection

listitem.para: Copy the generated JDBC URL, driver class, username, and password into the Edge screens. From Edge workloads, use host.docker.internal rather than 127.0.0.1.
Prepare a local staging directory, for example /tmp/singine.
Sync the directory into the live Edge pod:
listitem.programlisting: python3 -m singine.command collibra edge files sync /tmp/singine --dest /tmp/singine

listitem.para: Verify the staged files in the pod:
listitem.programlisting: python3 -m singine.command collibra edge files ls /tmp/singine

listitem.para: For a different local directory, repeat the same pattern and choose a matching remote destination:
listitem.programlisting: python3 -m singine.command collibra edge files sync /tmp/my-dataset --dest /tmp/my-dataset

refsect1.title: Operational semantics

listitem.para: Remote destination path inside the pod. The command preserves the relative file layout beneath the source directory.
Print the sync plan without writing to the pod.

refsect1.para: This workflow stages files into pod-local writable storage, not a persistent host mount. If the target pod is recreated, run the sync again.

refsect1.title: Examples
refsect1.programlisting:
# Stage the default sqlite payload python3 -m singine.command collibra edge files sync /tmp/singine --dest /tmp/singine
# Stage a single file under an explicit path python3 -m singine.command collibra edge files sync /tmp/singine/sqlite.db --dest /tmp/singine/sqlite.db
# Preview what would be copied without mutating the pod python3 -m singine.command collibra edge files sync /tmp/singine --dest /tmp/singine --dry-run
# Inspect the remote directory afterwards python3 -m singine.command collibra edge files ls /tmp/singine
# Print PostgreSQL datasource values for the Edge UI python3 -m singine.command collibra edge create datasource connection
